content: The orthographic and lexical ambiguity of the spelling variant 'reflexion' (a less common alternate spelling of 'reflection' taught in high school English vocabulary curricula) can be leveraged to bootstrap more robust LLM self-reflection mechanisms by prompting the model to evaluate its own outputs from two deliberately separated 'reflexion' vs 'reflection' frames: one focused on surface-level literal reflex of input patterns, and one focused on deep critical self-reflection of reasoning. This dual-frame prompt forces the LLM to contrast inconsistent solution perspectives, aligning with the core insight of existing self-reflection research.
